title: CNNs for Image Tasks
description: A non-technical client asks why CNNs work so well on images. Explain in plain language.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I keep hearing CNN. What is it, in plain words?

templates.text: Convolutional Neural Network. It's the kind of model people use for images.
templates.hint_zh: 卷積神經網路。是處理影像最常見的那種模型。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Why is it specifically good for images?

templates.text: It looks at small patches first, then combines them. Like noticing edges, then shapes, then objects.
templates.hint_zh: 它先看小區塊,再組合起來。先邊緣、再形狀、再物件。

templates.text: A regular one would have too many weights and lose the local structure of the image.
templates.hint_zh: 一般網路權重會太多,而且會丟掉影像的局部結構。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What about Transformers? I heard they're taking over?

templates.text: Vision Transformers do compete now. CNNs still win when data is small or compute is limited.
templates.hint_zh: Vision Transformer 確實能競爭了。資料少或算力有限時,CNN 仍占優勢。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: So for our project, which would you pick?

templates.text: I'd start with a CNN. We can move to a Transformer later if quality plateaus.
templates.hint_zh: 我會先用 CNN。之後品質卡住再換 Transformer。
